stories.title: Lagarde floats early ECB exit to play role in French presidential campaign
stories.summary: PARIS — European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said she could leave her post before the end of her term to take part in France’s 2027 presidential election. Asked in an interview with French daily Les Echos published Thursday if she could step down early to “play a role” in the vote next year, the former French economy minister said it was “possible.” “A European voice must be heard in the presidential debate,” she said. Lagarde did not say what role she would seek if she returned to

stories.title: EU conservatives attempt family therapy in Washington
stories.summary: Some conservative European politicians are trying to rescue a relationship with President Donald Trump that has become surprisingly rocky over the past few months. They got their chance as about 20 right-leaning EU lawmakers came to Washington for a long-scheduled visit that has become particularly urgent since trans-Atlantic tensions spiked with the Iran war. “We have to overcome the frictions between the president and our prime minister, for sure,” said Carlo Fidanza, an Italian

stories.title: Failing to deter Putin would cost more than paying for defense, Estonian PM warns EU
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU must do more to fund its own security and step up support for Ukraine, Estonia’s prime minister said as Baltic leaders meet in Berlin to discuss the threats facing the bloc. Speaking ahead of the meeting with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Friday, Kristen Michal told POLITICO “the question is no longer whether Europe should do more for its own security. The question is how quickly we can deliver. A stronger Europe means a stronger NATO.” The EU is locked in a debate

stories.title: Hungary revokes asylum of Polish politicians taken in by Orbán government
stories.summary: Marcin Romanowski and Zbigniew Ziobro arę both wanted at home on charges of corruption and embezzlement of public funds, among others.

stories.title: How many more Lyhannas must there be before France takes child sexual assault seriously? | Rokhaya Diallo
stories.summary: The outrage following the 11-year-old girl’s killing is justified. But the case reveals a deeper systemic neglect of child protection When the body of an 11-year-old girl was found in a disused grain silo on a farm in the Gers region of south-west France last month, the news sent shockwaves across the country . Lyhanna had been missing for nearly a week . Members of the public had been out combing the area. Suspicion quickly focused on Jérôme Barella, the 41-year-old father of one of Lyhanna’s c

stories.title: Földi Károly elmondta, miért támogatta közel százmillió forinttal a Tiszát
stories.summary: A vecsési cégtulajdonos már két évvel a választás előtt megígérte Magyar Péternek, hogy jelentős összeggel fogja segíteni, de volt egy feltétele.

stories.title: Európai munkanélküliség: a fő szám szépen javul, de a fiataloknak nem könnyű munkát találni
stories.summary: Magyarország a kedvezőbb adattal rendelkező országok között van, a lengyelek robognak, meglepő módon, a finneknél a legrosszabb a helyzet.

stories.title: Nagy ugrás a hazai fizetési piacon: Itthonról szolgálja ki egész Európát az indiai Pay10
stories.summary: Fontos és jövőbe mutató történet búvik meg egy egyszerű MNB-bejelentés mögött. Sikeres indiai cég jön Európába, de ami a lényeg, hogy mindezt Magyarországon keresztül teszi.

stories.title: Monacói otthona előtt hajtottak végre merényletet a szankcionált ukrán oligarcha ellen
stories.summary: A támadásban Vadim Jermolajev súlyosan megsérült, feleségének amputálni kellett a lábát.

stories.title: Meghalt Szilágyi Tibor színész
stories.summary: Szilágyi több mint 150 színházi szerepet játszott el, emellett több mint 120 filmben és tévés produkcióban szerepelt.

stories.title: Magyar rendőrök is részt vesznek egy nők bedrogozására és megerőszakolására szakosodott nemzetközi online hálózat felszámolásában
stories.summary: Európai nyomozók egy olyan hálózatra bukkantak, amelynek tagjai online csevegőszobákban cserélnek tapasztalatot, képeket és videókat a szexuális bántalmazásokról.

stories.title: Ezekre az ügyekre gondolhatott Magyar Péter, amikor közpénzhorgásznak nevezte Áder Jánost
stories.summary: A miniszterelnöknek valószínűleg Áder klímavédelmi alapítványának állami támogatása és az exállamfői rezidencia szúrhatja a szemét.

stories.title: A Tate fivérek azzal viccelődtek, hogy a romániai szervezett bűnözés és terrorizmus elleni ügyészség egy exkluzív klub, ahova pezsgőzni mentek
stories.summary: Andrew és Tristan Tate jelenleg szabadlábon vannak, miután a bukaresti bíróság áprilisban megszüntette a hatósági felügyeletüket, sőt úgy határozott, hogy a perköltségeiket is átvállalja a román állam.

stories.title: Megrázhatja a magyar akkumulátoripart, ha távozik a Semcorp
stories.summary: A Semcorp által gyártott szeparátorfóliák esetében sok nehézséggel jár a szállítás és a raktározás, így az ezeket felhasználó akkumulátorgyáraknak kellemetlen lehet, ha távozni kényszerül a vállalat.

stories.title: Hungarian police part of operation dismantling international online network specializing in drugging and raping women
stories.summary: European investigators have uncovered a network whose members exchange experiences, photos, and videos of sexual abuse in online chat rooms.

stories.title: Tusk: Lengyelország erőteljesen készül egy orosz provokációra
stories.summary: A lengyel kormányfő szerint az elkövetkező hónapok kritikusak lesznek.

stories.title: 2022 és 2026 között több mint ötvenszer ült magánrepülőre Szijjártó Péter, közel ötmilliárd forint ment el az útjaira
stories.summary: A külügyminisztérium összesítése szerint a korábbi miniszter számára évről évre több gépet béreltek az utazásaihoz.

stories.title: Alleged Scattered Spider hacker arrested in Finland
stories.summary: The 19-year-old dual American-Estonian national has been extradited to the US, where he is facing federal charges.

stories.title: Poland orders Swedish submarines to bulk up navy
stories.summary: Poland has placed an order for three Swedish submarines in an effort to step up defense capabilities. The NATO member is modernizing its navy in the face of Russian aggression at its borders.

stories.title: Spyware used against MEP investigating Pegasus abuses, report finds
stories.summary: Researchers say Stelios Kouloglou’s device was compromised after he joined European parliamentary committee NSO Group’s hacking software was repeatedly used against a member of the European parliament while he was conducting an investigation of spyware abuses in Europe, according to a new report. Researchers at the Citizen Lab at the University of Toronto said they could not attribute the attacks against Stelios Kouloglou to any particular government operator of Pegasus spyware. But their invest

stories.title: First Thing: Iran to show defiance in six-day funeral for supreme leader
stories.summary: Ceremony for Ali Khamenei intended to be epic display of national power. Plus, the expected wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce pays dividend to good causes Good morning. Final preparations are under way for Ali Khamenei’s six-day funeral . The farewell to the former supreme leader is expected to draw millions in Iran. Khamenei was killed in the opening salvo of the US-Israeli attack on the country in February, and the funeral is intended to be an epic display of personal mourning, national

stories.title: Probe finds former MEP investigating Pegasus was hacked with Pegasus
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Former member of the European Parliament Stelios Kouloglou was “repeatedly hacked “with Pegasus spyware while serving on the Parliament’s committee investigating spyware abuses, according to a new forensic investigation . The revelation raises fresh questions about whether the work of the inquiry itself was compromised. Researchers from Toronto University’s Citizen Lab found that Kouloglou’s phone was infected at least twice — on Oct. 21, 2022 and March 6

stories.title: Italy joins Bulgaria in resisting EU sanctions against Putin ally Patriarch Kirill
stories.summary: Italy has joined Bulgaria in voicing concerns about plans to sanction Russia’s Orthodox Patriarch Kirill, according to three EU diplomats informed on the deliberations. Chief EU diplomat Kaja Kallas has proposed an EU visa ban for Kirill, an ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin who has repeatedly praised and justified Moscow’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, as part of the EU’s 21 st package of sanctions against Russia. But the move has run into objections from Bulgaria, which has a substant

stories.title: ‘Ridiculous’ for US to maintain current Nato support, Trump warns ahead of alliance summit
stories.summary: President says Washington’s relationship with Nato is ‘not reciprocal’ and ‘they were not there for us’ in Iran war Donald Trump has said it is “ridiculous” for the US to continue its “one sided” relationship with Nato, less than a week before a summit of the military alliance in Ankara. Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform that “They were not there for us!!!” and Washington’s relationship with Nato “is not reciprocal”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Jacinta Allan admits criminals infiltrated Big Build but rejects calls for royal commission
stories.summary: Premier apologised over organised crime in some of Victoria’s largest construction projects, in op-ed that claimed a royal commission would not solve the issue Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Jacinta Allan has admitted that criminals have infiltrated some of Victoria’s largest construction projects – but has again rejected growing calls for a royal commission into alleged corruption involving unions and labour hi

stories.title: Turkish comedian arrested for 'insulting' Islam and Erdogan
stories.summary: Turkish comedian Deniz Göktaş was arrested at Istanbul Airport on Thursday following a stand-up show posted to YouTube. The 32-year-old faces multiple probes, including "publicly insulting religious values" and insulting President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

stories.title: ‘Never looked any cleaner’: US envoy shrugs off Brussels park damage claims
stories.summary: U.S. Ambassador to Belgium Bill White insisted Brussels’ Parc du Cinquantenaire was left spotless after his lavish celebration marking 250 years of American independence, as Belgian authorities probe whether fireworks damaged one of the capital’s historic buildings. “We are in immediate contact with the Belgian company that was hired to coordinate all event logistics, including the fireworks company,” White wrote on X on Friday. He said the contractor would “remediate where it is req

stories.title: Moira Deeming wins temporary reprieve as Victorian Liberal party postpones decision on her future
stories.summary: State opposition tells court it will not take any steps to disendorse MP while legal proceedings under way Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Victorian Liberal MP who sued her party to stave off a meeting that will determine her political future has been handed a temporary lifeline. Moira Deeming launched an eleventh-hour supreme court challenge against the Victorian Liberal party president, Brian Loughnane, and t

stories.title: Ali Khamenei’s six-day funeral expected to draw millions in Iran
stories.summary: Huge scale of funeral for supreme leader across five cities is intended to relay message of resistance to rest of the world In the small hours of Friday the police roadblocks, stalls, posters and army vans were starting to appear across Tehran as millions of Iranians prepared to attend the long-delayed six-day funeral ceremony for Ali Khamenei’s, Iran’s supreme leader for 36 turbulent years. Khamenei was killed in the opening salvo of the US-Israeli attack on the country in February, and the fun

stories.title: Kill zones and drone nets: a journey through Ukraine’s fortress belt
stories.summary: A strategic line of towns and cities is crucial to Ukraine’s defence – and where the war is at its most brutal A vast cobweb of spent fibre-optic cable is draped over the buildings in the small Ukrainian city of Lyman. Used to control the deadly drones deployed by both Russia and Ukraine, it has accumulated so densely after the years of fighting here that fresh drones struggle to fly through it, rotors tangling in the mass. Birds pluck it out to make their nests. Beneath the glistening strands,

stories.title: Details of MCG assault against Lidia Thorpe revealed after court lifts suppression order
stories.summary: Ebony Bell convicted and handed community work order following assault on senator and second ‘gratuitous act of violence’ while on bail Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A woman has been handed a community work order for punching Lidia Thorpe outside the MCG over claims the independent senator disrespected her mother. Ebony Bell was initially told to undertake an anger management course after her 2024 attack on Tho

stories.title: ‘I can only describe it as a war zone’: the rescuers navigating Venezuela’s post-quake hellscape
stories.summary: Thousands of volunteers are joined by overseas teams in the hope of finding more survivors in the rubble, reports Tom Phillips in Caraballeda When twin earthquakes tore through Venezuela’s northern coast last week, Israel Rivas was at home hundreds of miles away in the industrial city of San Félix. As the scale of the catastrophe became clear, the 24-year-old knew he had to react. A mechanic and budding photographer, Rivas gathered the money he had been saving to buy a new camera lens and jumped

stories.title: Wildfires rage in parts of France and Spain after record heatwave – video
stories.summary: Numerous wildfires are raging across parts of France and Spain, after record temperatures in Europe caused dry conditions and a worsening drought. Two thousand firefighters battled several wildfires fanned by strong winds along France's Mediterranean coast on Thursday, while Spain’s military emergency unit (UME) said it had deployed more than 100 personnel and about 40 vehicles to support firefighting efforts in the Aragon region Weather tracker: Heatwave breaks June temperature records across E

stories.title: Democrats will have ‘field day’ with Trump inquiries if they win House, legal experts say
stories.summary: Investigations into president and corruption charges will get heavy scrutiny if Democrats win majority in midterms Donald Trump’s presidency is facing investigations and corruption charges from a key House Democrat and ex-prosecutors, involving political and personal abuses of power, which legal experts predict will get heavy scrutiny if Democrats win the House majority in the midterms. Legal critics call the scandals dogging the president “target rich” for investigations that Democrats will hav

stories.title: UK summer bookings jump as Britons put off overseas holidays by travel fears
stories.summary: Reports of ‘stampede’ for stays near water amid concerns over cancelled flights, higher air fares and EU border delays Summer bookings at Britain’s hotels and holiday parks have jumped, compared with last year, as fears about flight cancellations and long delays at EU borders have prompted many UK holidaymakers to stay closer to home this year. There has been a surge in last-minute bookings for UK holidays amid warnings that airlines will have to raise their fares because of higher jet fuel bill

stories.title: Ukraine: Russia strikes kill 4 as Kyiv holds day of mourning
stories.summary: The overnight strikes by Russia came a day after massive missile and drone attacks on Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, where a day of mourning is being observed.

stories.title: Monaco IDs suspect in bomb attack on Ukrainian oligarch
stories.summary: Authorities have issued an Interpol Red Notice and arrest warrant against a woman from Ukraine identified as suspect in a bombing that injured a Ukrainian-born businessman sanctioned by Kyiv and two other people.

stories.title: Right-wing AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
stories.summary: Germany's right-wing AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

stories.title: Air conditioner rush: Scuffles break out at Lidl stores across France
stories.summary: Hundreds of shoppers queued outside Lidl supermarkets across France on Thursday after the retailer announced the sale of 200,000 fans and portable air conditioners, triggering long lines, scuffles and police intervention at several stores.

stories.title: Venezuela's interim leader defends earthquake response as death toll tops 2,500
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez has hit back at criticism over her government’s response to the quakes, rejecting accusations of slow rescue efforts and claims that official casualty figures have been under-reported.

stories.title: Belgian government under fire after heat wave claims 1,200 lives
stories.summary: Belgium’s federal government is facing mounting criticism after health authorities linked last week’s record-breaking heat wave to over 1,200 excess deaths, prompting opposition parties to accuse ministers of failing to protect the country’s most vulnerable citizens. Socialist Party leader Paul Magnette led the criticism Thursday evening after the health ministry confirmed 1,222 excess deaths between June 18 and June 29. “1222 deaths during the recent heat wave episode, a 39 pe

stories.title: Moldova’s prime minister resigns after series of scandals
stories.summary: Moldovan Prime Minister Alexandru Munteanu announced his resignation on Friday, putting an end to the pro-European government he led since last November. “I accepted the proposal to be Prime Minister with great responsibility and with the firm belief that I can contribute to changing things for the better,” he wrote in a post on his Facebook page. “But the moment I understood that I could no longer exercise my mandate in accordance with the principles and beliefs I have, I chos

stories.title: Race to control wildfires in southern France – in pictures
stories.summary: Nearly 3,000 people have been evacuated in southern France as the country swelters through a record-breaking heatwave. The fire started at a campsite, destroying dozens of mobile homes before spreading to the marina area, where thick, toxic smoke blanketed boats. The fire broke out in the town of Sainte-Marie-la-Mer and spread to Canet-en-Roussillon on Thursday [• This gallery’s headline and subheading were amended on 3 July 2026; an earlier version referred to south-western France ] Continue re

stories.title: China says man who flew plane into Beijing skyscraper had mental health problems
stories.summary: Official statement offers the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual and fatal incident in Beijing Chinese authorities said the man who flew a small plane into Beijing’s tallest skyscraper last week was a 66-year-old who had mental health problems. The statement published on Thursday offered the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual incident that occurred in Beijing’s central business district on the evening of 26 June. Continue reading...

stories.title: NSW records first suspected case of deadly H5 bird flu as virus reaches Australia’s east coast
stories.summary: Migratory giant petrel discovered near Hawks Nest north of Newcastle infected with H5. Testing under way to determine if it’s highly pathogenic H5N1 strain Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast New South Wales has its first suspected case of the deadly H5 bird flu in a giant petrel that was found near Hawks Nest, north of Newcastle, on the state’s coast. If CSIRO testing confirms it is the highly pathogenic H5N1 strain

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Both sides vow to escalate fighting after Russia’s deadly Kyiv barrage
stories.summary: Zelenskyy says his forces will ‘definitely’ retaliate over Russian bombardment of capital as Kremlin vows to ramp up pressure. What we know on day 1,591 Continue reading...

stories.title: NSW shadow upper house leader steps aside during Icac Liberals inquiry – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A teenager has been charged with murder after a 15-year-old boy was discovered with fatal stab wounds outside a community medical centre. AAP reports the boy was found critically injured outside Craigieburn Community hospital in Melbourne’s north about 7.50pm on Wednesday. A staff member from the clinic rushed to his aid and delivered CPR but he died at the scene. Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave breaks June temperature records across Europe
stories.summary: UK provisional peak of 37.7C shatters previous record by huge margin, while Germany hits all-time high of 41.7C Last week’s heatwave across western Europe shattered national June records and set new all-time highs. The UK recorded a provisional high of 37.7C at Lingwood in Norfolk on Friday 27 June, smashing the previous June record of 35.6C, set in 1976. Such a margin is exceptionally rare: temperature records are typically broken by 0.1C or 0.2C, not a remarkable 2.1C. Continue reading...

stories.title: Zohran Mamdani to deliver speech to mark US’s 250th birthday in New York
stories.summary: New York mayor’s remarks at city hall to come just hours before Trump is set to deliver address at Mount Rushmore Zohran Mamdani, the mayor of New York City, is expected to deliver a speech on Friday morning to mark America’s 250th birthday. The address, scheduled to begin at 10am local time and streamed live, will be delivered from behind George Washington’s desk in New York city hall. Mamdani will be surrounded by recently naturalized citizens. Continue reading...

stories.title: Spain’s conservatives and far-right Vox increase ties with Andalucía coalition
stories.summary: Deal including ‘national priority’ policy brings prospect closer of countrywide agreement between parties The prospect of a national coalition between Spain’s conservative People’s party (PP) and its far-right Vox party has drawn closer still after the two groupings sealed another deal that will allow the PP to continue ruling the southern region of Andalucía. The PP, which has governed the former socialist bastion for the past seven years, lost its absolute majority in May’s regional election ,

stories.title: How baby formula recalls have mounted amid Trump staff cuts to the FDA
stories.summary: Experts say the Food and Drug Administration is not prepared for the health threat of bacterial contamination Multiple brands of infant formula have been recalled recently due to bacterial contamination, and experts say the Food and Drug Administration is inadequately prepared to deal with the health threat they pose in the wake of Trump administration cuts. Last March, the FDA announced the launch of Operation Stork Speed , specifically intended to “expand options for safe, reliable, and nutrit

stories.title: UK parents warned over posting images of children amid AI sexual abuse fears
stories.summary: Exclusive: National Crime Agency and safety watchdog issue guidance amid rise in explicit material online AI prey: why watchdogs are telling parents to protect children from nudification apps The UK National Crime Agency has recommended parents should not put photos of their children on public display online as part of landmark guidance to tackle the rise of AI-generated sexual abuse material. Advice issued by the NCA and the child safety watchdog the Internet Watch Foundation suggests parents a

stories.title: Starling Bank to cut 130 jobs and boost investment in AI to reduce costs
stories.summary: The London-based fintech says restructuring is necessary to reduce ‘duplicate’ roles Business live – latest updates Starling Bank has said it will cut more than 100 jobs as it invests more heavily in artificial intelligence to push down costs. The digital-only bank told staff that 3% of its workforce, or 130 jobs, would be made redundant, as part of a restructuring of its banking and tech operations. Continue reading...

stories.title: Downing Street defends decision to let pubs stay open until 5am for England match – UK politics live
stories.summary: The government said it had to react to England’s progression in the World Cup, which was only confirmed on Wednesday Downing Street said it would continue to use X after the culture secretary, Lisa Nandy, announced she would stop using the social media platform. Nandy said yesterday the culture and media department will also stop using X because the site “now favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful debate”. It is for individual departments to decide what is right for them in this regard

stories.title: Flowers, food and a fairytale castle: New York preparations point to giant Taylor Swift wedding
stories.summary: Singer and fiance Travis Kelce have been coy but festivities appear to be getting under way at Madison Square Garden In her hit song Welcome to New York, Taylor Swift sings: “Like any great love, it keeps you guessing.” The megastar has certainly done that this week, as preparations began for what appears to be a giant wedding at Madison Square Garden in New York . Vans unloading flowers, food and what looks like the makings of a massive, white fairytale castle, have been seen outside the arena

stories.title: Ukrainian woman suspected of Monaco parcel bombing was ‘disguised as a man’
stories.summary: Suspect seen in Germany after attack apparently targeting tycoon Vadym Iermolaiev The main suspect in a Monaco bomb attack this week that seriously injured a Ukraine-born business tycoon and two of his family members is a Ukrainian woman living in Germany who disguised herself as a man, authorities have said. Interpol, the international police organisation, on Friday issued a red notice for Anastasiia Berezovska , aged 39, describing her as German-speaking with dark hair and a tattoo, possibly o

stories.title: Venezuela leader rejects criticism for earthquake response
stories.summary: Acting President Delcy Rodríguez has slammed critics for saying her government was slow to respond to the disaster. She said the criticism was "manufactured in propaganda laboratories."

stories.title: AI drones made in Germany see duty on Ukraine's front line
stories.summary: The German defense company Helsing is supplying combat drones to Ukraine and will soon provide them to the German army. How effective are they on the front line? DW joined a Ukrainian combat mission to find out.

stories.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
stories.summary: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodies adapt?

stories.title: Man dies after setting himself on fire outside UN headquarters in New York
stories.summary: A man has died after setting himself alight outside the UN headquarters in New York. Activists identified him as a Tibetan activist, though authorities have not confirmed his identity or motive.

stories.title: Pakistan: Bus crashes into ravine, killing at least 40
stories.summary: Authorities say the overcrowded bus was likely speeding when it veered off a mountain road in Balochistan.

stories.title: India news: Indian delegation leaves for Khamenei's funeral
stories.summary: A team of Indian delegates has left for Iran to attend the state funeral of Iran's former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali ​Khamenei. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Germany news: Workers protest Mercedes-Benz cost-cutting
stories.summary: Thousands of Mercedes-Benz workers across Germany are protesting the carmaker's cost-cutting drive. Meanwhile, firefighters rescued two window cleaners from a faulty platform high above Frankfurt. DW has more.

stories.title: Iran: Dignitaries arrive for Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
stories.summary: Officials from China, Russia, India and other countries are in Tehran as Iran prepares to mourn the Supreme Leader, who was killed in an airstrike at the start of the US-Israeli campaign against Iran.

stories.title: Moldova: Prime Minister Munteanu resigns
stories.summary: Alexandru Munteanu announced his surprise resignation after just eight months as head of the government. President Maia Sandu said a successor would be appointed "quickly" in order to continue Moldova's pro-EU course.

stories.title: Germany: Voters are skeptical of reform proposals
stories.summary: The German government is aiming to get the country back on track with a wide range of reforms. However, the latest Deutschlandtrend survey shows that the public remains unconvinced.

stories.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
stories.summary: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

stories.title: Sudan: Catastrophe unfolding in el-Obeid: UN rights envoy
stories.summary: As summary executions, abductions, torture and sexual violence plague el-Obeid, Sudan, the UN's human rights chief said the world must take action to stop preventable atrocities.

stories.title: Can inspectors return to Iran's nuclear sites?
stories.summary: The UN nuclear watchdog says inspections of Iran's nuclear program are possible in principle. The bigger question is whether Tehran will allow meaningful access.

stories.title: South Africa, Ghana clash over migrant's death
stories.summary: The two countries are embroiled in a diplomatic dispute following the death of a Ghanaian national in South Africa this week. It comes amid rising tensions in South Africa over undocumented migration.

stories.title: Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
stories.summary: The ads use terms including “rape” and “child video” and link to content on the messaging app Telegram.

stories.title: 6.2 earthquake hits eastern Indonesia
stories.summary: The tremor hit at a depth of 120 kilometres, roughly 58 kilometres west of Tobelo in North Maluku province, and officials confirmed there is no tsunami threat.

stories.title: 'Most massive' Russian attack on Kyiv kills at least 30
stories.summary: The latest barrage deployed the largest number of weapons on the capital and hit locations over a wide area.

stories.title: Israelis mark 1,000 days since 7 October attack
stories.summary: Thousands gathered in Tel Aviv to mark 1,000 days since the 7 October 2023 attack, as former hostages and bereaved families addressed the crowd.

stories.title: Venezuela quake survivor pulled out alive after eight days
stories.summary: Hernán Gil was trapped under a collapsed multi-storey car park.

stories.title: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills nine, Syrian state media say
stories.summary: There was no immediate claim for the bombing at a cafe close to the Palace of Justice, a major government building.

stories.title: Firefighters battle major wildfire in southern France
stories.summary: A major wildfire in France's Aude department burns 900 hectares as aircraft and firefighters battle the blaze and evacuees reach emergency shelters.

stories.title: Portugal fans celebrate World Cup win over Croatia
stories.summary: Thousands of Portugal fans gathered in central Lisbon to celebrate a 2-1 victory over Croatia, sending their team through to the World Cup last 16.

stories.title: Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February
stories.summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.

stories.title: Rescued bile bears released in Denmark forest
stories.summary: Six rescued Asiatic black bears from South Korea start a new life in Denmark’s Knuthenborg Safaripark after an international rescue operation.

stories.title: At least 32 dead after overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in Pakistan
stories.summary: The bus was travelling from Quetta to Peshawar, when it crashed in the Dana Sar mountain range.

stories.title: Ebola treatments trial begins in DR Congo
stories.summary: According to WHO data, there have been 1,406 confirmed cases of the disease in DRC, with 301 suspected cases and 438 deaths.

stories.title: Dogs don Argentina shirts as World Cup fever grips Buenos Aires
stories.summary: A dog walker in Buenos Aires leads dogs dressed in Argentina jerseys through the city as World Cup fever grows and fans celebrate in the streets.

stories.title: France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather
stories.summary: Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.

stories.title: Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope

stories.title: Iran to host dozens of foreign leaders for Khamenei's funeral, with Western nations absent
stories.summary: Russia's Medvedev, Pakistan's prime minister and Taliban officials are among roughly 30 country delegations expected as Iran buries slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei this week.

stories.title: Ukrainian woman named by Interpol as main suspect in Monaco bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials in Monaco say they believe she was "disguised as a man" and may not have acted alone.

stories.title: Video shows moment 11-year-old drives pickup truck into Buddhist procession
stories.summary: Ten people were killed after an 11-year-old boy drove a pickup truck into a Buddhist procession in northeastern Thailand.

stories.title: Von der Leyen unveils major EU support initiatives in strategic trip to Armenia
stories.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a significant economic and political support package to Armenia during her strategic visit to engage with the South Caucasus peace process and the new opportunities in the region formerly in Russia’s orbit.

stories.title: Ryanair warns of 'queue chaos' from new EU border system
stories.summary: The airline calls on European governments to postpone the EES system until after the summer holiday period.

stories.title: Russia's fuel crisis: Is Putin under pressure?
stories.summary: Ukraine's extended campaign against Russian energy infrastructure is disrupting fuel supplies and military logistics. With shortages mounting in occupied Crimea, is the pressure pushing Putin toward negotiations?

stories.title: Canadian boy dies of rabies after waking to find bat on his face
stories.summary: Eleven-year-old developed symptoms 19 days after encounter in Ontario in ‘exceedingly rare’ case Doctors in Canada say a child who awoke to find a bat resting on his nose and mouth while visiting an Ontario cottage later died of rabies, in an “exceedingly rare case” that highlights the need for better public awareness. In a report published this week in the Canadian Medical Association Journal, infectious disease physicians confirmed that the 11-year-old boy died from rabies, a fatality they sai

stories.title: Venezuelan man saved from collapsed mall eight days after earthquakes
stories.summary: Security guard Hernán Alberto Gil Flores, 43, initially told rescuers not to tell his wife in case he did not survive A 43-year-old security guard who survived last week’s devastating earthquakes in Venezuela thanks to a pocket of air in his workstation cabin has been pulled from the collapsed basement of a shopping centre amid huge cheers from international rescue teams. Hernán Alberto Gil Flores had been trapped for eight days under the rubble of the Galerías Playa Grande in the hard-hit coast

stories.title: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
stories.summary: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipient As Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister. Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. Continue reading...

stories.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
stories.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

stories.title: German public university creates Islamic theology faculty
stories.summary: The University of Münster is the first public university in Europe to establish an Islamic theology faculty, a move that is is attracting international attention.

stories.title: German coalition agrees on changes to pensions, tax rates
stories.summary: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government plans to push through sweeping changes with the stated goal of reviving a sluggish economy. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

stories.title: Russia launches attacks on Kyiv, killing at least 27 and injuring scores
stories.summary: Ballistic and cruise missiles and drones were used in what Kyiv's mayor, Vitali Klitschko, said was the largest attack on the city since the war began.

stories.title: Cheers and applause as security guard rescued from rubble
stories.summary: Emergency workers managed to free Hernán Gil more than 100 hours after they had first located him under 140 tonnes of rubble.

stories.title: Residents of Ethiopian town forced to kill hundreds of their own dogs after rabies deaths
stories.summary: Some residents reluctantly hung their dogs to avoid being arrested or fined, locals tell the BBC.

stories.title: Iran renews Hormuz threats as Doha talks end with cautious progress
stories.summary: Tehran threatened a "forceful response" against vessels straying from its approved Hormuz routes, even as mediators said indirect US-Iran talks in Doha concluded with "positive progress". The next round of talks will wait until after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral, which begins on Saturday.

stories.title: French aerobatic team stages flypast over New York for US anniversary
stories.summary: Patrouille de France flies over New York with Alpha Jets and coloured smoke during celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

stories.title: UN to close its human rights office in Burkina Faso following junta's suspension
stories.summary: Junta chief captain Ibrahim Traoré has pursued anti-Western policies since seizing power in a coup in September 2022, while critical voices are met with repression.

stories.title: Venezuelan security guard pulled alive from basement eight days after twin quakes
stories.summary: The earthquakes destroyed tens of thousands of buildings across northern Venezuela, killing more than 2,200 people, and leaving La Guaira state as the hardest-hit region in the country.

stories.title: Vatican excommunicates followers of global Catholic sect
stories.summary: Around 600,000 followers of the Society of Saint Pius X, a Catholic sect, are affected.

stories.title: Killers of British couple in South Africa sentenced to life
stories.summary: The pair had been collecting rare plants and seeds before their bodies were found days later in a river.

stories.title: Daredevil couple charged with felonies for Empire State Building proposal stunt
stories.summary: The highest public floor is the 102nd, where there’s an observation deck. Going higher requires a key card, according to the court complaint.

stories.title: Former Olympian indicted on felony charge over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
stories.summary: District of Columbia US Attorney Jeanine Pirro said David Hearn ripped up recently installed sealant on pool in “a deliberate act” that caused more than €875 in damage.

stories.title: Former Olympian indicted for allegedly vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool
stories.summary: US authorities charged him with property destruction after renovations championed by President Donald Trump appeared to go awry.

stories.title: A mayor in Japan announced her maternity leave - and got the whole country talking
stories.summary: In the face of criticism, Shoko Kawata, 35, says she loves her job and is proud to be taking time off to have a baby.

stories.title: After 250 years the American Dream is surviving, but only just
stories.summary: The American Dream has persisted since the days of the Founding Fathers, but faith in the ideal is fading

stories.title: On the Strait of Hormuz, BBC finds seized ships and shark fishermen as uneasy calm returns

stories.title: What will guests wear to Taylor Swift's wedding?
stories.summary: Romantic, fairytale vibes could dominate Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's reported wedding event in New York City on Friday.

stories.summary: Itt a következő hét napra szóló Telex Diszpó.

stories.title: Az Origo 550 milliónyi állami hirdetést kapott fél év alatt tavaly
stories.summary: A Mandinernek 390 millió, a Borsnak 245 millió jutott.

stories.title: Betöltheti-e a hangüzenet az űrt, amit a cseteléssel teremtettünk egymás között?
stories.summary: Sokan utálják, mások szeretnek hangüzenetben kommunikálni. Tari Annamária pszichoterapeuta segítségével jártunk utána, mit veszítünk azzal, ha csak cseten tartjuk egymással a kapcsolatot, és javít-e a helyzeten a hangüzenet.

stories.summary: Johan Manzambi ragyogó szóló után hozta kihagyhatatlan helyzetbe a csapattársát. Svájc kényelmes, 2-0-s győzelemmel jutott be a nyolcaddöntőbe.

stories.summary: A kutatócég június végi mérése szerint a választani tudó szavazók között 67-24-re vezet a Tisza.

stories.summary: A férfi a kórházba szállítás után belehalt sérüléseibe.

stories.title: Nem tetszik a bíróknak, hogy nekik kell majd elküldeni a Kúria és az Országos Bírói Hivatal elnökét
stories.summary: Szerintük a kormány javaslata nem veszi figyelembe, hogy milyen kiszolgáltatottak a bírók.

stories.summary: A horvát válogatott csapatkapitánya szerint szabályos gólt vettek el tőlük a meccs végén.

stories.summary: Egyelőre leginkább az előző generáció paródiáinak tűnnek, akik épp eltévedni készülnek a Bermuda-háromszögben. Van-e árapály a Tiszán? Létezik-e viccesebb politikus Deutsch Tamásnál? Miért vannak öten a Fidesztikus Négyesben a címlapképünkön? Megannyi kínzó kérdés, amire egy kivétellel választ is adunk.

stories.title: Novemberben dönthet az új klímatörvényről az Országgyűlés
stories.summary: Az alkotmánybíróság tavaly nyáron június 30-ig adott határidőt az Alaptörvény-ellenes klímatörvény módosítására.

stories.summary: Az előfizetéses konstrukcióban adott szemüvegre Európában csak egy igazán sikeres kísérletet találtunk, Skandináviában.

stories.summary: Annyira komolyan vették a fenyegetést, hogy még Teheránt is figyelmeztették.

stories.title: Elindultak a csúszdán a lakáshitelek kamatai – de hol állhatnak meg?
stories.summary: Júliustól megkezdődött a kamatcsökkentés a lakáshiteleknél is. Elsőként a legnagyobb jelzálog-hitelező, az OTP Bank lépett: fél százalékot faragott több lakáshitelének a kamatából is. Mivel a bankok árazását meghatározó referenciakamatok hónapok óta csökkennek, a BiztosDöntés.hu szerint az ajánlatait kedvezőbbé tevő OTP-t várhatóan a többi bank is követi majd hamarosan.

stories.title: A Volkswagen kiszállhat az FC Bayern Münchenből és a VfB Stuttgartból
stories.summary: Nincs könnyű helyzetben, szerkezetátalakításra és elbocsátásokra készül a VW-csoport. Ennek keretében értékes futballrészvényeket is eladna a cég.

stories.summary: Minden évben van egy időkapu, amikor egyszerűen lehet váltani a biztosítók között, vagy biztosítón belül a termékek között.

stories.summary: A másfél és tizennyolc év közötti gyerekek közül többen beszélni sem tudtak, az idejük nagy részét egy kis szobába zárva tölthették.

stories.summary: Az elemző szerint a megújulás egyelőre teljes hiánya elvesztegetett idő az ellenzéki párt számára.

stories.summary: Péntek este a New York-i Madison Square Gardenben, több mint ezer meghívott előtt összeköti az életét Amerika álompárja. Ezt csak pletykákból lehet tudni, a sztárpár ugyanis a jelek szerint az esküvő minden részletét vasszigorral próbálja titkolni. Több-kevesebb sikerrel, ugyanis pár dolog így is kiderült.

stories.summary: Papp Lászlót a miniszterelnök is lemondásra szólította fel, de ő közölte, hogy marad, és a kormányhivatalra mutogat.

stories.title: A világbajnoki kudarc után lemondott a német válogatott szövetségi kapitánya
stories.summary: Julian Nagelsmann elvesztette a szövetség bizalmát.

stories.title: Jövőre már nem a Velencei-tónál rendezik meg az EFOTT-ot
stories.summary: A szervezők egyelőre nem árulták el, hova költözik a fesztivál.

stories.title: Vádat emeltek a megye IV-es focimeccsen kitört tömegverekedés ügyében
stories.summary: A Mernye és az Igal játékosai lökték, ütötték és rúgták egymást, majd beszálltak a nézők is.

stories.summary: Az amerikai védelmi miniszter végül csak nekiment az európai NATO-tagoknak.

stories.summary: Az országszerte kedvelt szentesi paradicsom- és paprikaföldek mellé óriási csirkevágóhidat telepítenek, ami teljesen ellehetetleníti a biogazdálkodást. A város nem segít a termelőnek, a gyár pedig csak annyira, amennyiből nem tud továbblépni.

stories.title: A hőhullám legdurvább napjai alatt közel 30 százalékkal nőtt a halálozások száma Franciaországban
stories.summary: Június utolsó hetében 2025 többlethalálesetet regisztráltak az országban.

stories.summary: A gyárat hetente többször ellenőrzik szúrópróbaszerűen, miután a tűzvédelmi hatóság több súlyos szabálytalanságot talált.

stories.summary: Az Oscar-díjas színésznő szerint a filmben elhangzó párbeszédek is olyanok voltak, mintha egy számítógép írta volna őket.

stories.summary: A Biodómban még van mit fejleszteni hulmánerőforrás-menedzsmenten.

stories.summary: Az Anglia–Mexikó-meccs miatt a kormány hajnali ötig engedélyezte a pubok működtetését, a rendőrök szerint túl későn jött a döntés.

stories.title: Côte d’Ivoire floods kill 59 as west Africa endures torrential rains
stories.summary: Authorities say rainy season getting deadlier, with Ghana reporting 13 dead and floods hitting Benin, Togo and Nigeria Floods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, the communication minister told a cabinet meeting in Abidjan. There are fears the toll could further rise as rescue teams continue to search for victims during the rainy season, which runs from May until July, the minister, Amadou Coulibaly, added. Continue reading...

stories.title: Explosion in Monaco: Who is Vadym Yermolaiev?
stories.summary: Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was reportedly injured in a suspected assassination attempt in Monaco. Kyiv had previously imposed sanctions on him. What is known about the case?

stories.title: Turkish comedian Deniz Goktas detained at Istanbul airport
stories.summary: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

stories.title: Nicolás Maduro sued in US court over alleged extrajudicial killings in Venezuela
stories.summary: Maduro is currently in a New York jail awaiting trial on criminal charges of drug trafficking after the US military removed him from office in an extraordinary raid in Venezuela in January.

stories.title: Alleged member of hacking group 'Scattered Spider' arrested in Finland
stories.summary: Scattered Spider, also known as “Octo Tempest,” “UNC3944,” or “0ktapus,” is a group of criminal cyber actors that attempts to extort ransoms from companies.

stories.title: Smoke rises over Kyiv after Russian overnight strikes
stories.summary: Smoke rises over Kyiv after a large-scale Russian missile and drone attack overnight that killed at least eight people and injured dozens.

stories.title: South Africa and Ghana in diplomatic row over alleged killing of migrant
stories.summary: South Africa denies claims that a Ghanaian citizen was killed in anti-migrant protests in Cape Town.

stories.title: Andy Burnham is about to have a crash course in hardcore geopolitics | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: The likely next prime minister would rather be in Warrington than Washington, but foreign policy will dominate his agenda more than he thinks If Andy Burnham is lucky, he will get some time to adjust to the pace of life as prime minister before dealing with his first international crisis. It won’t be long. Donald Trump is an engine of constant turmoil. Aftershocks from his recent military adventure in the Middle East will be felt for years. None of the declared war aims were achieved. The Irania

stories.title: Sudan’s RSF committed crimes against humanity in El Fasher, Amnesty says
stories.summary: Report accuses paramilitary force of crimes including ethnic cleansing in systemic campaign against civilians The Sudanese paramilitary Rapid Support Forces committed crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing during its campaign to capture El Fasher, Amnesty International has alleged. Many of the crimes, including murder, torture, rape, enslavement and sexual slavery, were carried out as part of a widespread and systematic attack against civilians and amounted to crimes against humanity, the

stories.title: Will alleged crime ties damage Serbia's ruling party?
stories.summary: Pressure on President Aleksandar Vucic is growing as years of investigations into alleged links between the state and organized crime coincide with a gang war. Experts say the issue is eroding support within his base.

stories.title: Ahead of NATO summit, Turkey arrests more than 200 activists
stories.summary: Protesters are clearly unwelcome ahead of the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara. Amid a strict public gatherings ban, 225 human rights and environmental activists as well as journalists have already been arrested.

stories.title: EU urged to exempt UK from car rules that could be worst Brexit impact yet
stories.summary: Bloc’s ‘made in Europe’ regulations risk shutting out British manufacturers in ‘most spectacular own goal in history’ The EU’s car industry has called for the UK to be fully included in new “made in Europe” rules that threaten to shut out British manufacturers from their biggest export market. stories.title: Four people die in mass fan celebrations in Mexico City after World Cup victory
stories.summary: Three of the victims died from suffocation More than a million people gathered in Mexico City Four people died, three ⁠from suffocation, as thousands of fans crowded Mexico City streets during World Cup celebrations, the capital’s health secretariat ⁠said in the early ⁠hours ​of Wednesday. The deaths occurred near the Angel of Independence landmark, where thousands of fans had gathered to ⁠celebrate Mexico’s 2-0 victory over Ecuador in the last 32. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump refuses to renew US-Canada-Mexico trade pact he once championed
stories.summary: Trump and US officials opted to keep USMCA alive on short leash of annual reviews rather than longer term renewal Donald Trump has refused to renew the North American trade pact he once championed as his signature deal, opting instead to keep it alive on a short leash of annual reviews rather than committing to another 16 years. Wednesday was the deadline built into the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) for the three countries to jointly decide its fate, which is set to expire in 203

stories.title: China’s ethnic unity law denounced as ‘forced assimilation’ by rights groups
stories.summary: Law comes into effect that critics fear will further erode rights of Uyghurs and Tibetans, as well as allow Beijing to pursue dissidents abroad A new ethnic unity law has come into effect in China despite warnings from Taiwan, the United Nations and rights groups that it could threaten freedoms, especially for minorities. stories.title: Understanding Ebola’s wildlife origins is crucial to preventing next big outbreak
stories.summary: If we don’t know the source, not only do humans remain at risk but wildlife can suffer needlessly via retaliation While virologists and public health departments were palpitating over the news of an Andes virus infectious disease outbreak on a cruise ship (13 cases, three deaths), in the Democratic Republic of the Congo the Bundibugyo virus, the root of the current Ebola outbreak (currently more than 1,250 cases and at least 362 deaths), was smouldering under the radar. Bundibugyo virus is a hor

stories.title: Young Indonesian couple publicly caned after kissing on TikTok
stories.summary: Unmarried man and woman whipped 21 times each because they had violated province’s version of Islamic law A young couple in Indonesia’s conservative Aceh province have been publicly caned after a Sharia court convicted them of violating Islamic law by kissing during a TikTok livestream. The court ordered the couple, a 22-year-old man and a 25-year-old woman, to be whipped with a rattan cane 21 times each for kissing without being married. At least 100 people witnessed the caning, carried out by

stories.title: At least nine monks killed in Thailand after boy drives truck into procession
stories.summary: Charges yet to be filed over incident in Mukdahan involving 11-year-old, as police seek to establish circumstances An 11-year-old boy has driven his parents’ truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing at least nine monks. CCTV footage shared by a local rescue group showed the moment the monks, wearing orange robes, were run over as they walked in procession along a road. The timestamp on the footage was shortly before 11am local time on Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires

stories.title: US declines to renew USMCA trade pact with Mexico, Canada

stories.title: Hospital fire in northern Germany kills two
stories.summary: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. Many patients and staff were evacuated from the facility.

stories.title: Why US trains trips are slower and worse than they should be
stories.summary: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

stories.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
stories.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

stories.title: Vatican excommunicates rebel SSPX bishops, followers
stories.summary: The Vatican's watchdog authority has imposed severe disciplinary measures on an ultratraditionalist Catholic group. The move comes after the group, the Society of Saint Pius X, consecrated bishops without papal consent.

stories.title: Planned 1.7 million satellites 'devastating' for astronomy by making night sky brighter
stories.summary: There are also concerns that huge amounts of space debris from satellites could increasingly crash into each other in a dangerous chain reaction known as "Kessler syndrome."

stories.title: Ukrainian charged in Germany over Nord Stream blasts
stories.summary: Ukraine denies involvement in the case which may have serious implications for its relationship with Germany.

stories.title: US blocks long-term renewal of North American trade deal
stories.summary: US blocks 16-year North America trade deal renewal, triggering annual rolling reviews

stories.title: California to institute Bruce Lee Day, first for a Chinese-American in state history
stories.summary: In the 1960s, Lee found work in Hollywood, notably as Kato in the TV series "The Green Hornet," but studios wanted him to play racist stereotypes and paid him less than his white counterparts.

stories.title: Banned by Beijing, this comedian is taking his act to Chinese speakers abroad
stories.summary: Chizi, one of China’s biggest stand-up comedians, has found a new stage after falling afoul of China's censors.

stories.title: From Truman's pension to Trump's billions - a White House windfall unmatched by any president
stories.summary: Historians say Trump's $2.2bn income last year is unprecedented and blurs the line on conflicts of interest.

stories.title: From friendship bracelets to wedding rings: A Taylor and Travis timeline
stories.summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.

stories.title: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.title: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

stories.title: US-Iran parallel talks in Qatar end with pledge to keep talking
stories.summary: Holding separate meetings in Qatar, the US and Iran made progress and agreed that the next meeting would be after Khamenei's funeral, host Qatar confirmed.

stories.title: Canadian boy, 11, dies of rabies after waking to bat on his face
stories.summary: Rabies infections are rare in Canada. There have been 28 human deaths due to rabies in the country since 1924.

stories.title: Venezuela's death toll at least 2,295 as medical crisis widens
stories.summary: Doctors fear a severe medical crisis in Venezuela following the 24 June earthquakes that killed 2,295 and injured more than 11,000. Thousands of displaced survivors are living in crowded shelters without clean water.

stories.title: 'Beautiful and terrible' - the world's thoughts on the US at 250
stories.summary: From Brazil to India to Egypt, the BBC asked people across the world what they think of America today.

stories.title: Gaza patients face agonising delays for medical evacuation
stories.summary: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

stories.title: People smuggler convicted in France found by BBC living in UK and seeking asylum
stories.summary: Once described as "the godfather" of French migrant camps, he has been working in a Leicestershire village.

stories.title: Russia's petrol crisis worsens as citizens feel the bite of Moscow's war in Ukraine
stories.summary: A third of Russia's refining capacity has been knocked out by Ukrainian drone attacks, causing petrol shortages across more than half of Russia's regions.

stories.title: Couple climbs Empire State Building, gets engaged, then arrested
stories.summary: Two climbers scaled the Empire State Building's antenna, displayed a peace banner and appeared to get engaged before being arrested by New York police.

stories.title: US fans celebrate World Cup last-16 spot
stories.summary: US supporters celebrate in Carson, California, after the United States beat Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to reach the World Cup round of 16.

stories.title: China says pilot crashed small plane into skyscraper for 'personal reasons'
stories.summary: The 66-year-old, who died in the crash, had anxiety and referenced "ending his life" in his diary.

stories.title: Kazakhstan starts living by new Constitution, sets date for parliamentary elections
stories.summary: Kazakhstan's new Constitution entered into force on July 1, prompting the Kazakh President to set August 23 as the date for parliamentary elections required under the new constitutional framework.

stories.title: Fast-moving wildfire spreads across southern France
stories.summary: Firefighters battle a fast-moving wildfire in Aude, southern France, after flames spread from Hérault and burned more than 800 hectares overnight.

stories.title: Any deal with Tehran is 'a pause before the next war,' Reza Pahlavi tells Euronews
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview with Euronews, Reza Pahlavi said the framework agreement would allow the Islamic Republic to regroup and rearm, with the only winner from the deal being the regime in Tehran.

stories.title: Nine Thai monks killed after 11-year-old driver collides with procession
stories.summary: The group of 35 monks and five lay followers were walking by the roadside when the truck ploughed into them.

stories.title: 11-year-old boy dies from rabies in Canada after waking up to a bat on his face
stories.summary: Rabies is a rare but deadly infection usually spread by a bite or scratch from an infected animal. It is almost always fatal after the onset of symptoms, although vaccination and early treatment can help to prevent it.

errors.error: <urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
<urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
